title: [lora_l2.c:361-362] Device pointer captured once but deinit may invalidate
description:
What: rx_thread() captures lora_data.lora_dev once at startup (line 361) under mutex, then uses this cached pointer throughout its lifetime. However, deinit() does not clear lora_data.lora_dev.

Why it matters: If deinit() + init() happens with a different device (e.g., hot-swappable radio on some hypothetical hardware), the RX thread would use a stale device pointer. In practice this is low risk because:
1. deinit() requires stop() first, which terminates the RX thread
2. Subsequent init() would generate a new thread with fresh pointer
3. LICHEN targets fixed-radio embedded systems

This is a theoretical concern rather than practical bug given current architecture.

Evidence:
```c
// rx_thread startup
k_mutex_lock(&lora_mutex, K_FOREVER);
const struct device *dev = lora_data.lora_dev;  // captured once
k_mutex_unlock(&lora_mutex);
// ... uses 'dev' for all lora_recv() calls
```
close_reason: Documented safety: stop() joins RX thread before returning, deinit() only callable after stop() completes. Added comment at lora_l2.c:369-385 explaining the shutdown sequence ensures the cached lora_dev pointer cannot become stale.
